fact: March 2 is exactly 61 days into the year, which means there are exactly 304 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, March 2 typically falls during the time when maple trees start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes maple syrup!
March 2 often marks the peak of 'kitten season' in warmer climates when outdoor cats start having babies because spring is approaching!
Around March 2, the sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day in most northern places, so you're gaining daylight faster than you can blink!
March 2 sits right in the sweet spot where people born on this day are Pisces - the fish sign that's supposedly the most creative and dreamy of all zodiac signs.
Gross but cool: earthworms usually start wiggling up to the surface around March 2 in warmer places because the soil is finally thawing out from winter!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, March 2 is typically when autumn really kicks in and leaves start turning brilliant orange and red.
March 2 falls exactly 18 days before the spring equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal lengths all over Earth.
In cold northern regions, March 2 is often when hibernating bears start stirring in their dens, getting ready to wake up hungry and grumpy!
When there's a full moon on March 2, it's called the 'Worm Moon' because that's traditionally when worms start popping up everywhere in northern climates!
In northern climates, March 2 typically marks the time when ice on frozen lakes and ponds starts making weird creaking and popping sounds as it begins to crack and melt.
In many temperate regions, March 2 is usually when you first start seeing those tiny green buds on tree branches that look like little alien eggs about to hatch!
